headline: What it will take for Big Tech to wow investors this earnings season
summary: US stock futures (ES=F, NQ=F, YM=F) got a lift in Monday's pre-market trading, coming off of last week's chip sell-off as investors await to hear earnings results from Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), Tesla (TSLA), Intel (INTC), and IBM (IBM) this week. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Breaking News Reporter Jake Conley and Bullseye American Ingenuity Fund portfolio manager Adam Johnson to discuss what investors will be paying close attention to when it comes to Big Tech's investments and returns on AI.

US stock futures (ES=F, NQ=F, YM=F) got a lift in Monday's pre-market trading, coming off of last week's chip sell-off as investors await to hear earnings results from Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), Tesla (TSLA), Intel (INTC), and IBM (IBM) this week.

Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Breaking News Reporter Jake Conley and Bullseye American Ingenuity Fund portfolio manager Adam Johnson to discuss what investors will be paying close attention to when it comes to Big Tech's investments and returns on AI.

headline: Magnificent Seven Dominate S&P 500 Earnings, But That's Changing Dramatically
summary: The Magnificent Seven will again reign supreme over S&P 500 earnings, but their dominance is fading.  As second-quarter earnings reports start to flow in, analysts expect the Magnificent Seven companies to grow earnings by a combined 31.1%.  The other 493 S&P 500 companies are expected to increase earnings 22.8%, FactSet Senior Earnings Analyst John Butters wrote in a report.

content.title: Magnificent Seven Dominate S&P 500 Earnings, But That's Changing Dramatically
content.summary: The Magnificent Seven will again reign supreme over S&P 500 earnings, but their dominance is fading.  As second-quarter earnings reports start to flow in, analysts expect the Magnificent Seven companies to grow earnings by a combined 31.1%.  The other 493 S&P 500 companies are expected to increase earnings 22.8%, FactSet Senior Earnings Analyst John Butters wrote in a report.

headline: Wall Street Saw Its Strongest Week 1 Earnings in Over 3 Years
summary: Bank of America strategists recapped the first week of earnings saying that with 50 S&P 500 companies reports in, 88% beat expectations.  UBS' CIO office also flagged stronger than historical averages for both beat rates and EPS growth, saying that the stock market has further to run thanks to what's expected to continue to be a strong earnings season.  "The wide gaps between individual stock performance and ongoing risks related to geopolitics and inflation, however, mean that investors should ensure diversified exposure," the strategists wrote.

content.title: Wall Street Saw Its Strongest Week 1 Earnings in Over 3 Years
content.summary: Bank of America strategists recapped the first week of earnings saying that with 50 S&P 500 companies reports in, 88% beat expectations. "The wide gaps between individual stock performance and ongoing risks related to geopolitics and inflation, however, mean that investors should ensure diversified exposure," the strategists wrote.

headline: Stocks Wobble to Kick Off a Big Tech Earnings-Heavy Week
summary: The Dow was down 300 points or 0.6% after reversing earlier gains.  Chip stocks led the declines, with the same names leading the market year-to-date seeing the most dramatic losses.  The Big Tech results could further catalyze the rotation out of tech or jump start a rebound from a rout that’s run too far.

headline: Tesla (TSLA) Faces A New Fremont Neighbor As Agility Opens Robot Training Center
summary: Agility Robotics has opened a humanoid robot training center near Tesla's upcoming Optimus factory in Fremont. The facility sits next to the planned Tesla Optimus site, highlighting growing activity in humanoid robotics for industrial use. The development spotlights potential competition and collaboration between Tesla (NasdaqGS:TSLA) and Agility in advanced robotics. Tesla is best known for electric vehicles and energy products. The Optimus humanoid robot project signals a broader push...

headline: Strong earnings growth needed to justify tech valuations, says expert
summary:
STORY: Citing Alphabet's Google and Elon Musk's Tesla as examples, Brown said that "to justify the high valuations that we see in a lot of those names, we would need to see good earnings growth, not just kind of your run-of-the-mill 10%, but you'd want to see higher earnings growth on top of good margins as well."

The second-quarter earnings season will pick up the pace this week, with results due from big names like Alphabet, Tesla, and Intel, broadening out the picture they provide of the health of corporate America.

headline: Stock Market Today: Major Indexes Fade, End Lower to Begin Week; Oil Rises as Trump Says Iran ‘Will Pay’ for Killing Soldiers
summary: Major stock indexes finished lower to begin a busy week of earnings, while oil prices rose after President Donald Trump said Iran “will pay” for the deaths of American soldiers.

content.title: Stock Market Today: Major Indexes Fade, End Lower to Begin Week; Oil Rises as Trump Says Iran ‘Will Pay’ for Killing Soldiers
content.summary: Major stock indexes finished lower to begin a busy week of earnings, while oil prices rose after President Donald Trump said Iran “will pay” for the deaths of American soldiers.

thumbnail.caption: Shares of AMC Entertainment surged 26% Monday after it reported record revenue.Credit: Michael Nagle / Bloomberg via Getty Images

headline: US stocks edge down with Iran and earnings season in focus
summary:
STORY: Stocks started the week on a down note, with the Dow falling about six tenths of one percent, while the S&P 500 dropped two tenths and the Nasdaq ended basically flat.

:: Archive

Investors looked for moves toward de-escalation in the Middle East while they waited for earnings reports due from major technology companies later in the week.

Melissa Brown, managing director of investment decision research at SimCorp, says the technology firms will have to post significant earnings growth to maintain their lofty trading levels. 

“Every company, whether it's Google or Tesla, obviously they have very different business models and different drivers of their business. But overall, I think to justify the high valuations that we see in a lot of those names, we would need to see good earnings growth, not just kind of your run-of-the-mill 10%, but you'd want to see higher earnings growth on top of good margins as well. So, we want the businesses to be profitable and growing.”

:: Archive

Meanwhile, Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis said on Monday that they were imposing a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, opening a new front in the U.S.-Iran war and widening the threat to global energy supplies and trade beyond the Gulf. 

But a senior Iranian official told Reuters that mediators have passed Iran a proposal to de-escalate the war with the U.S. that would offer a 10-day ceasefire to find ways to revive an interim deal reached last month.

Stocks on the move included Paramount Skydance which lost two percent and Warner Brothers Discovery which slipped almost four percent after a judge halted their $110 billion merger temporarily as a coalition of states argued it would irreparably harm competition.

And shares of Domino’s Pizza gained two percent after the chain’s quarterly revenue edged past Wall Street estimates.
